title: Issues, Reviews, Pull Requests, and Team Workflow · Command Recall
summary: Move a change from issue to review to integration with traceable decisions, bounded branch scope, and respectful collaboration. Learners retrieve rules and procedures before seeing feedback. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for a team contribution and review protocol after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Choose trunk-based, feature-branch, release-branch, or fork collaboration from delivery, risk, ownership, and release constraints.
Connect issue context, acceptance criteria, branch scope, commits, tests, and review evidence without making the tracker the source code.
Prepare a focused review range, description, changed-case evidence, migration notes, and explicit reviewer questions.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-basics cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Retrieve the complete evidence cycle
steps.instruction: Order the Workflow Policy evidence cycle for this review task before implementing it.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Workflow Policy assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward.

content.text: Issue 5 requires one behavior and accessibility regression. A pull request contains five unrelated cleanup commits; define review range, split scope, required evidence, and authorized merge policy. Competency probe: Choose trunk-based, feature-branch, release-branch, or fork collaboration from delivery, risk, ownership, and release constraints.

content.title: Ordering constraint
content.text: Order work so Workflow Policy has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.

hints: Name the Workflow Policy precondition or invariant that the review case must preserve.

Trace this concrete case before choosing: Issue 5 requires one behavior and accessibility regression. A pull request contains five unrelated cleanup commits; define review range, split scope, required evidence, and authorized merge policy. Competency probe: Choose trunk-based, feature-branch, release-branch, or fork collaboration from delivery, risk, ownership, and release constraints.
Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: One branching model is best for every team and release cadence.

options.text: Inspect repository boundary, config origin, current operation, worktree, index, refs, and graph for the review Workflow Policy case
Preserve uncommitted work and a recovery ref, then state intended tree and topology for the review Workflow Policy case
Preview and perform the least destructive simulated Git operation for the review Workflow Policy case
Verify status, staged and range diffs, graph, tests, remote target, and recovery path for the review Workflow Policy case

review case — Issue 6 requires one behavior and accessibility regression. stimulus.title: Workflow Policy failure evidence
stimulus.caption: A plausible visible result conflicts with the governing contract and requires a cause-level repair.

lines.label: observed
lines.text: One branching model is best for every team and release cadence.

lines.label: required
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Choose trunk-based, feature-branch, release-branch, or fork collaboration from delivery, risk, ownership, and release constraints.

options.text: For workflow policy · changed case, apply Workflow Policy without its stated preconditions, omit the a team contribution and review protocol trace and counterexample, and treat one plausible output as complete proof. This does not satisfy Workflow Policy for a team contribution and review protocol.

Choose trunk-based, feature-branch, release-branch, or fork collaboration from delivery, risk, ownership, and release constraints. Apply it to the stated input and retain a reproducible trace for a team contribution and review protocol.

stimulus.title: Integration Policy case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: evidence

options.text: The Integration Policy record says a team contribution and review protocol looks right but supplies no governing condition, observation, changed case, or counterexample. This does not resolve separate evidence from confidence.

The record names the assumption, applies Integration Policy, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.

steps.title: Record what required effort
steps.instruction: Write the review evidence note for Integration Policy: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — Issue 2 requires one behavior and accessibility regression. A pull request contains five unrelated cleanup commits; define review range, split scope, required evidence, and authorized merge policy. Competency probe: Apply approvals, required checks, merge queue, update policy, merge method, and branch cleanup consistently.
steps.why: Retrieving Integration Policy through the concrete a team contribution and review protocol case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Apply approvals, required checks, merge queue, update policy, merge method, and branch cleanup consistently.

checks.description: The evidence cycle runs from problem framing through changed-case validation.
checks.failureMessage: Frame the decision before modeling, and validate after obtaining a result.

checks.description: The decision applies Workflow Policy to the changed stakeholder case.
checks.failureMessage: Recheck the conditions and reject the documented misconception about Workflow Policy.
